title: Prospects for Improving the Regulatory Process Using E-Rulemaking
summary: U.S. citizens participate directly in rulemaking—a deliberate agenda-setting process designed to elicit, sort, and clarify fact and opinion from a wide variety of interested parties.
body: U.S. citizens participate directly in rulemaking—a deliberate agenda-setting process designed to elicit, sort, and clarify fact and opinion from a wide variety of interested parties. The rulemaking process offers a directness and scope for public participation unavailable in other policy-making processes. To view full text please see PDF below (login may be required).
